refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the customer-defined evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is capable of performing its intended mission to total operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it explicitly defines the measurement basis (availability ratio), the target threshold (≥0.97), the evaluation period scope, and acknowledges that availability is a system-level emergent property dependent on vehicle reliability, teleoperation robustness, safety service responsiveness, and maintenance effectiveness. High safety criticality demands that availability not be achieved by masking failures; instead, it must be supported by fault detection, graceful degradation, and rapid recovery.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry collection and post-mission analysis: track system state transitions (operational, degraded, maintenance, safety-hold), log failure events with timestamps and root causes, compute availability as (total_time - downtime) / total_time, compare against target threshold. Acceptance requires ≥0.97 over the agreed evaluation period (e.g., 30 days of field operation).
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve ≥0.95 mean time between failures (MTBF) or equivalent reliability metric.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain ≥0.99 network link uptime and command-response latency within safety-critical thresholds.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall detect and respond to hazardous conditions within defined response time budgets without introducing spurious shutdowns.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE Solution shall implement predictive maintenance scheduling and fault isolation to minimize unplanned downtime.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant subsystems (dual Vehicle units, backup Teleoperation links, safety service failover) with automatic switchover logic.
DP-B: Implement continuous health monitoring, predictive diagnostics, and scheduled maintenance optimization to maximize mean time between failures and minimize repair time.

DP-C: Implement a hierarchical fault tolerance architecture with graceful degradation modes (e.g., reduced-speed operation, manual override fallback) that keeps the system operational under partial failures.

DP-D: Implement a combination of DP-B and DP-C: proactive health monitoring with predictive maintenance, coupled with graceful degradation and fault isolation to achieve availability targets without full redundancy.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement a combined health monitoring and graceful degradation architecture. Specifically: (1) continuous telemetry collection and anomaly detection across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to predict failures before they occur; (2) scheduled maintenance optimization based on predictive analytics to minimize unplanned downtime; (3) graceful degradation modes (e.g., reduced operational envelope, manual fallback, single-operator mode) that allow continued mission execution under partial failures; (4) rapid fault isolation and recovery procedures to restore full capability. This approach balances cost, complexity, and safety criticality by avoiding full redundancy while maintaining high availability through early detection and controlled degradation.

potential_sources: Health monitoring system must integrate telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services (moderate coupling, but well-defined via Telemetry interface).
Graceful degradation logic must coordinate with Safety Services to ensure degraded modes do not violate safety constraints (high coupling, mitigated by explicit safety service integration).
Predictive maintenance scheduling must account for operational tempo and customer availability windows (moderate coupling, managed via Tasking interface).
coupling_check.notes: DP-D exhibits expected couplings inherent to system-level availability management. Couplings are manageable through well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) and clear ownership boundaries. No hidden or problematic decoupling identified.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Telemetry data quality and latency are sufficient to enable predictive diagnostics (risk: poor sensor calibration or network delays could degrade prediction accuracy).

Assumption: Graceful degradation modes are acceptable to the customer and do not violate mission requirements (risk: customer may require full capability at all times, making graceful degradation infeasible).
Assumption: Maintenance windows can be scheduled without excessive operational disruption (risk: high-utilization sites may have limited maintenance windows, increasing unplanned downtime).
Risk: Safety Services must not introduce false positives that trigger unnecessary shutdowns, as this would degrade availability (mitigation: rigorous validation of safety service logic).

Risk: Predictive maintenance algorithms may require significant historical data and tuning; initial deployment may not achieve full benefit (mitigation: phased rollout with continuous model refinement).
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but should be refined to explicitly define: (1) the telemetry data model and collection frequency required for predictive diagnostics; (2) the graceful degradation modes and their safety constraints; (3) the maintenance scheduling algorithm and its integration with customer operational calendars; (4) the fault isolation procedures and recovery time budgets. These refinements will be addressed in the DP -> IM mapping and lower-level decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary that orchestrates health monitoring, predictive maintenance, graceful degradation, and fault recovery across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). It owns the availability KPI and is responsible for implementing the combined health monitoring and graceful degradation architecture (DP-D). The module includes: (1) system-level telemetry aggregation and anomaly detection logic; (2) predictive maintenance scheduling and optimization; (3) graceful degradation mode selection and coordination; (4) fault isolation and recovery orchestration.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Includes health metrics (MTBF, latency, error rates), sensor data, and system state information. Used for anomaly detection and predictive diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional tasking interface with Site Manager and Tele-operator. Inbound: mission requests and operational tempo. Outbound: availability status, maintenance scheduling recommendations, graceful degradation mode notifications.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional safety alert interface with Safety Services. Inbound: hazard detections and safety constraints. Outbound: graceful degradation mode confirmations and fault isolation requests that respect safety boundaries.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network connectivity for telemetry transmission, maintenance scheduling updates, and remote diagnostics. Critical for predictive maintenance and health monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of the availability KPI and system-level health monitoring. DP-D (combined health monitoring and graceful degradation) is inherently a system integration concern that belongs at this level. Minimal diffusion: only the graceful degradation mode selection logic may require coordination with Vehicle and Safety Services, but this is managed via well-defined interfaces.

Vehicle owns its own reliability (MTBF) and fault detection, but does not own system-level availability orchestration, predictive maintenance scheduling, or graceful degradation mode selection. Assigning DP-D to Vehicle would scatter system-level logic across subsystems, violating separation of concerns.

Teleoperation owns network link uptime and command-response latency, but not system-level availability management or cross-subsystem health monitoring. Assigning DP-D to Teleoperation would create inappropriate coupling and diffusion.

Safety Services owns hazard detection and safety constraints, but not system-level availability orchestration or predictive maintenance. Assigning DP-D to Safety Services would conflate safety management with availability management, violating separation of concerns and creating unacceptable coupling.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the correct owner of DP-D because: (1) availability is a system-level emergent property that requires orchestration across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) health monitoring, predictive maintenance, and graceful degradation are system integration concerns that belong at the top level; (3) NGHE Solution already owns the Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces, which are essential for coordinating graceful degradation and maintenance scheduling; (4) assigning DP-D to any subsystem would create inappropriate diffusion and coupling.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if too many system-level concerns are assigned to it. Mitigation: clearly define the boundary of NGHE Solution to include only availability orchestration, health monitoring, and graceful degradation; delegate subsystem-specific reliability and fault detection to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Risk: Graceful degradation mode selection requires coordination with Safety Services to ensure degraded modes do not violate safety constraints. This coupling must be managed carefully via the SafetyAlert interface. Mitigation: define explicit safety constraints for each graceful degradation mode and validate them during design review.

Risk: Predictive maintenance scheduling requires integration with customer operational calendars and maintenance windows, which may not be fully known at design time. Mitigation: design the maintenance scheduling algorithm to be configurable and adaptive, with feedback loops to refine predictions based on actual maintenance outcomes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: The NGHE Solution module assignment is sound. Recommended refinements: (1) explicitly define the internal structure of NGHE Solution to include a Health Monitoring subsystem, a Predictive Maintenance subsystem, and a Graceful Degradation Coordinator; (2) define the interfaces between these internal subsystems and the external subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (3) specify the data model for telemetry aggregation and anomaly detection; (4) define the graceful degradation modes and their safety constraints in detail.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (combined health monitoring and graceful degradation architecture) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP is logically sound: proactive health monitoring and predictive maintenance reduce unplanned downtime, while graceful degradation allows continued operation under partial failures. The DP is feasible given the available interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and does not introduce unacceptable complexity or cost. Coupling is manageable and well-understood.

diffusion_risks: Graceful degradation mode selection logic must coordinate with Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface. This is a moderate coupling risk, but it is explicit and manageable.
Predictive maintenance scheduling must integrate with customer operational calendars via Tasking interface. This is a moderate coupling risk, but it is expected and manageable.
Health monitoring requires telemetry from all subsystems via Telemetry interface. This is a high-volume data flow, but it is well-defined and necessary for the DP to function.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct owner of DP-D. The module has high containment (0.95) and low diffusion (0.05). All external interfaces are well-defined and necessary for system-level availability orchestration. No hidden or problematic diffusion identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR (≥0.97 availability) is feasible via continuous telemetry collection and post-mission analysis. The NGHE Solution module will collect telemetry from all subsystems, compute availability metrics, and report them to the Site Manager via the Tasking interface. Acceptance testing will require ≥0.97 availability over the customer-defined evaluation period (e.g., 30 days of field operation).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the refined FR to the NGHE Solution module is clear. The module owns the availability KPI and is responsible for implementing the combined health monitoring and graceful degradation architecture. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through FR-NGHE-004.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively, with clear ownership boundaries.

recommended_revisions: Refine the graceful degradation modes and their safety constraints in detail. Define which operational capabilities are retained in each degraded mode and which are lost. Validate that degraded modes do not violate safety constraints.
Define the telemetry data model and collection frequency required for predictive diagnostics. Specify which metrics are collected from each subsystem and how often.
Define the maintenance scheduling algorithm and its integration with customer operational calendars. Specify how maintenance windows are negotiated and how unplanned downtime is minimized.
Define the fault isolation procedures and recovery time budgets. Specify how faults are detected, isolated, and recovered, and what the expected recovery time is for each fault type.

decision.justification: The refined FR, selected DP, and assigned IM are all sound and pass the gate reviews. However, the refined FR is not atomic; it has been decomposed into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through FR-NGHE-004.4) that require further decomposition. Additionally, the DP-D (combined health monitoring and graceful degradation architecture) is a high-level design concept that requires detailed specification of: (1) the telemetry data model and collection frequency; (2) the graceful degradation modes and their safety constraints; (3) the maintenance scheduling algorithm; (4) the fault isolation procedures and recovery time budgets. These details will be addressed in the next level of decomposition. Therefore, the decision is to ZIGZAG_DOWN and decompose the sub-FRs and DP-D into more detailed requirements and design parameters.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve ≥0.95 mean time between failures (MTBF) or equivalent reliability metric.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain ≥0.99 network link uptime and command-response latency within safety-critical thresholds.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall detect and respond to hazardous conditions within defined response time budgets without introducing spurious shutdowns.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE Solution shall implement predictive maintenance scheduling and fault isolation to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The NGHE Solution shall implement graceful degradation modes that allow continued mission execution under partial failures while respecting safety constraints.
FR-NGHE-004.6: The NGHE Solution shall implement continuous health monitoring and anomaly detection to enable predictive diagnostics and early fault detection.

assumptions: Telemetry data quality and latency are sufficient to enable predictive diagnostics.
Graceful degradation modes are acceptable to the customer and do not violate mission requirements.
Maintenance windows can be scheduled without excessive operational disruption.
Safety Services can be integrated with graceful degradation logic without introducing false positives.
Predictive maintenance algorithms can be developed and tuned based on historical data and field experience.
The customer-defined evaluation period is at least 30 days of field operation.
open_risks: Poor sensor calibration or network delays could degrade prediction accuracy.
Customer may require full capability at all times, making graceful degradation infeasible.
High-utilization sites may have limited maintenance windows, increasing unplanned downtime.
Safety Services may introduce false positives that trigger unnecessary shutdowns.
Predictive maintenance algorithms may require significant historical data and tuning; initial deployment may not achieve full benefit.
Graceful degradation mode selection logic must coordinate with Safety Services; this coupling must be managed carefully.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data model and collection frequency specification.
Graceful degradation modes and their safety constraints (detailed specification).
Maintenance scheduling algorithm and integration with customer operational calendars.
Fault isolation procedures and recovery time budgets.
Validation of graceful degradation modes against safety constraints.
Field trial data demonstrating ≥0.97 availability over the customer-defined evaluation period.
